stories.title: Jennifer Garner Describes ‘Interesting Relationship’ With the 2 Paparazzi Assigned to Her for More Than 20 Years
stories.summary: Jennifer Garner has a unique relationship with her assigned paparazzi, who have been following her for over 20 years. Two paparazzi are assigned to her daily, and she has developed an odd respect for them. This dynamic was revealed in a recent podcast interview, where Garner shared a personal story of feeling unsafe and seeking help from her main paparazzo. The incident highlights the complexities of the paparazzi culture that Garner has long advocated against.

stories.title: Caitlin Clark declines WNBA 3-Point Contest invitation, dealing major blow to All-Star Weekend and ESPN
stories.summary: WNBA star Caitlin Clark has declined an invitation to participate in the league's 3-Point Contest, a major blow to the event and its broadcast partner ESPN. Clark's participation was expected to draw at least a million viewers. The contest will now feature other participants without the league's most popular superstar. The event is scheduled to take place as part of the WNBA's All-Star Weekend.

stories.title: Gail Daughtry and the Celebrity Sex Pass Is Beautifully Implausible
stories.summary: A couple's pre-wedding fantasy takes a turn in David Wain's new film, Gail Daughtry and the Celebrity Sex Pass, where a woman's fiancé sleeps with his granted "hall pass" celebrity crush, Isabella Rossellini, in a small Kansas town. The comedy explores the potential consequences of such a scenario. The film, which premiered at Sundance in January, stars Zoey Deutch and Michael Cassidy. It delves into the complexities of relationships and the blurred lines between fantasy and reality.

stories.title: Internacional x Cruzeiro: onde assistir ao vivo, horário e escalações
stories.summary: In the Campeonato Brasileiro, Internacional faces Cruzeiro in a crucial match on Wednesday at 21h30 at the Beira-Rio stadium. Internacional seeks to improve its 14th place standing with 21 points, while Cruzeiro aims to climb the table from its 13th position. The match marks the return of both teams from the intertemporal break, with Internacional looking to bounce back from a 3-1 loss to Bragantino.

stories.title: Sporting Cristal x Red Bull Bragantino na Sul-Americana: onde assistir ao vivo, horário e escalação
stories.summary: Sporting Cristal faces Red Bull Bragantino in the Copa Sul-Americana playoffs on Wednesday at 21h30 in Lima, Peru. The match will be broadcast on Paramount. Sporting Cristal, third in their Libertadores group, aims to advance to the quarterfinals, while Bragantino, second in their group, seeks to secure a spot in the quarterfinals against Atlético Mineiro. The winner will have home-field advantage in the next round.

stories.title: Lego’s Donkey Kong arcade machine lets Mario jump endless barrels — Miyamoto is reportedly happy
stories.summary: A Lego Donkey Kong arcade machine set has been created, featuring a working game with a joystick and button controls. The set includes an endless series of barrels for Mario to jump, using the same original barrels first released in 1989 for Lego's pirate sets. The machine is fully functional, but does not play like the original Donkey Kong game. Mario creator Shigeru Miyamoto has reportedly expressed approval for the set.

stories.title: Even camels can't cope: Africa's ships of the desert hit by rising temperatures
stories.summary: Rising temperatures in Africa's Afar region are causing extreme heat stress in camels, with herder Ali Umer reporting eight camel calves have died in the past month due to blistering conditions. The region is one of the hottest and driest on Earth, where camels are used to carry salt through the desert. Temperatures are becoming increasingly intense, with even the wind now carrying heat. As a result, camel herders are struggling to care for their animals.

stories.title: Watch Internacional vs Cruzeiro Serie A game LIVE: Online streams, TV channel, early teams news, start time
stories.summary: Internacional hosts Cruzeiro in a crucial Serie A match on July 22, 2026, at 8:30 PM, with both teams desperate for points to avoid drifting further from safety. Internacional, 14th in the table, seeks to arrest a two-match losing streak, while Cruzeiro, 13th, looks to extend its unbeaten run in five outings. The match pits two of Brazil's storied clubs against each other, with Paulo Pezzolano's Internacional facing pressure to turn their season around. The outcome will have significant implications for both teams' Serie A standings.

stories.title: US signs landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
stories.summary: The US and Saudi Arabia have signed a landmark nuclear deal, a "peaceful nuclear co-operation agreement," allowing Saudi Arabia to develop a civilian nuclear program. The deal, announced on July 23, includes a bilateral safeguards agreement and could provide access for American companies in the Saudi nuclear energy program. The agreement lays the foundation for a decades-long, multi-billion-dollar partnership. The US Department of Energy will now monitor Saudi Arabia's nuclear activities to ensure nonproliferation.

stories.title: Canada's 'powerful' dairy sector is in Trump's trade crosshairs
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has targeted Canada's dairy supply management system, imposing a 50% tariff on $20 billion worth of Canadian goods imported to the US, set to take effect in August. The system, which includes production quotas and import quotas on dairy, eggs, and poultry, is a cornerstone of Canada's economy. Canadian politicians have indicated that dairy supply management is non-negotiable. The US-Canada trade dispute is expected to escalate further in the coming weeks.

stories.title: Oil extends gains as Trump threatens strikes on critical Iranian infrastructure
stories.summary: Oil prices surged 2% to $95.99 per barrel as US President Donald Trump threatened to bomb Iranian infrastructure in response to any attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz. This escalation comes after a recent incident where Iranian cruise missiles struck a tanker, disrupting crude supplies. Trump's warning targets one bridge or power plant per attack. The US and Iran have exchanged threats, raising concerns of further conflict in the region.

stories.title: Most sewage spills in England may be illegal, new research suggests
stories.summary: New research suggests that nearly 9 out of 10 hours of raw sewage spills in England may be illegal, with over 86% of incidents occurring without heavy rain. This analysis, based on data from over 350 storm overflow outlets, implicates all nine water companies serving more than 30 million people. The findings raise concerns about the legitimacy of sewage discharges. The water companies' practices are now under scrutiny.

stories.title: Hope for thousands of MS patients as 'life-changing' drug now on NHS in England
stories.summary: A life-changing drug for thousands of MS patients is now available on the NHS in England, with up to 5,000 people set to benefit each year. The medication, fampridine, boosts nerve signals to help people walk and live independently. Clinical trials showed nearly 40% of patients experienced improvements in walking speed and duration. This development could enable people to regain mobility and independence, with some already benefiting from the treatment.

stories.title: Airport drop-off fees up by a third - here are the priciest
stories.summary: Drop-off fees at the UK's major airports have risen by a third on average since last summer, with two London airports, Gatwick and Stansted, charging the highest fees at £10. The fees are part of airports' business models and climate change objectives, with London Gatwick's 10-minute fee being the highest. This comes as the airport tries to encourage more passengers to arrive by trains and buses. The Transport Secretary had previously required Gatwick to have 54% of passengers using public transport by the time a second runway is approved.

stories.title: Three Airports Plan to Ditch T.S.A. Amid Push for Private Security
stories.summary: The airports have opted in to the agency’s new security model, an agency spokesperson confirmed on Wednesday. A union official said the move could jeopardize public safety.

stories.title: Russia's businesses under strain from Ukraine's attacks on Wildberries
stories.summary: Ukrainian drones have hit two warehouses belonging to Russia's largest online retailer Wildberries, killing seven workers and injuring dozens in the Tambov region and near Moscow. The attacks, which also damaged goods worth an estimated £750m, target logistics hubs used by Russians to buy military equipment. This disruption affects hundreds of Russian sellers who depend on online marketplaces for their livelihoods. The war's impact is now being felt by small businesses and individuals, including a mother who lost £9,500 in the attack.

stories.title: McInnes glad of Rangers backing in appeal against ban
stories.summary: Rangers manager Derek McInnes has received a four-match touchline ban for criticizing a late penalty decision in a May match against Motherwell. The Scottish FA initially imposed a three-game ban with one suspended until the end of the season. McInnes is contesting the ban, stating the comments were directed at the decision, not officials. The appeal process is underway following a friendly victory over St Etienne at Ibrox.

stories.title: Google burning through cash with spiralling AI costs
stories.summary: Google's parent company Alphabet saw its free cash flow turn negative for the first time in at least a decade, coming in at $5.9 billion in the latest quarter. This is due to growing spending on artificial intelligence infrastructure, which is expected to reach $205 billion this year. Alphabet's combined quarterly revenue rose 23% to $119.8 billion, but the company's stock fell 4% in after-hours trading. The increased AI spending is part of a broader trend among major tech companies investing in the technology.

stories.title: Clarity Act Mired In Debate Over Whether to Bar President From Selling Crypto
stories.summary: Democrats and Republicans are haggling about the Clarity Act, a major bill pushed by the crypto industry, as it moves closer to a Senate vote.

stories.title: Yeon Sang-ho Returns to ‘Parasyte’ Universe With ‘Tamiya,’ Starring Shioli Kutsuna, at Netflix
stories.summary: South Korean filmmaker Yeon Sang-ho is returning to the "Parasyte" universe with a new live-action series, "Parasyte: Tamiya," starring Shioli Kutsuna, set to stream on Netflix in 2027. The series marks another collaboration between Netflix and Yeon, who developed the show and co-wrote the script with Ryu Yong-jae. This is Yeon's third project with Netflix, following "Hellbound" and "Parasyte: The Grey." The series is expected to premiere next year.

stories.title: Tesla Profit Falls Even as Car Sales Rebound
stories.summary: Tesla reported a $1.1 billion net profit in the second quarter, down from $1.2 billion a year earlier, despite a 25% increase in sales to $28.2 billion. The company's profit decline was attributed to lower margins on each sale. Tesla is investing heavily in new technologies and factories to produce batteries, semi-trucks, and other products. The company's sales growth is expected to continue, but at a slower pace due to global economic challenges.

stories.title: Shaken, Soaked and Seasick: What It’s Like to Survive ‘The Odyssey’ in 4DX
stories.summary: A 3-hour screening of Christopher Nolan's "The Odyssey" in 4DX format left some viewers shaken, soaked, and seasick. The immersive experience, featuring seat-rumbling and water-spritzing technology, was available at select theaters. At least one viewer reported feeling seasick during the film. The 4DX format is designed to simulate the sights and sounds of the movie, creating a more immersive experience.

stories.title: Tears and sleepless nights as Marshall and Adlington get Scotland flag honour
stories.summary: Scottish athletes Marshall and Adlington have been chosen to carry the Scotland flag at the Commonwealth Games. Marshall, a 59-year-old bowler, and Adlington, a 39-year-old judoka, will share the honour. The pair have been keeping the news secret for weeks, revealing it only to their closest family members. They will carry the flag at the opening ceremony, marking a special moment in the Games for both athletes.

stories.title: Liverpool confident of signing Barcola - Thursday's gossip
stories.summary: Liverpool is confident of signing 23-year-old Paris St-Germain forward Bradley Barcola, who impressed with four goal contributions at the World Cup with France this summer. The Reds aim to beat Arsenal and Bayern Munich to the signature. Talks are ongoing, with a potential transfer expected to be a significant boost to the team. Liverpool's pursuit of Barcola is expected to intensify in the coming weeks.

stories.title: Trump threatens to target Iran's bridges and power plants if Hormuz attacks persist
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has threatened to destroy Iranian bridges and power plants if attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz continue. This warning comes after 11 consecutive nights of US strikes on Iran, with Tehran warning against targeting its nuclear facilities. Trump's threat specifically targets structures located near the Iranian capital, Tehran. The US has already launched strikes on Iran's underground nuclear site near Natanz.

stories.title: ‘Doctor Strange’ Director Says Elon Musk ‘Doesn’t Know S— About Cinema’ After Musk Vows to Make AI ‘Odyssey’: ‘His Opinions on Art Are Worthless’
stories.summary: Elon Musk has vowed to create an AI-generated film of Homer's "The Odyssey" by the end of the year, using his tool Grok. The project has sparked criticism from film director Scott Derrickson, who called Musk's opinions on art "worthless" due to Musk's lack of understanding of cinema. Musk has been a vocal critic of director Christopher Nolan's "The Odyssey" since its release, citing "woke" casting decisions. The outcome of Musk's AI project remains to be seen.

stories.title: With Saudi Deal, Trump Loosens Nuclear Standards for One Close Ally
stories.summary: Nuclear experts say it throws fuel on the smoldering fire of atomic proliferation, creating a destabilizing situation that could raise the risk of nuclear war.

stories.title: Alphabet Quadruples Profit to $112 Billion, Fueled by A.I. Investments
stories.summary: Google’s parent company, which has spent heavily on artificial intelligence, also showed growth across its businesses, particularly in cloud computing.

stories.title: What Were the Lotus-Eaters Eating in Homer’s ‘Odyssey’?
stories.summary: In Homer’s epic, Odysseus’s men eat something so powerful that they forget about home. Which plants could have produced such a soporific, narcotic and amnesiac effect?

stories.title: Target ultra-processed food to reduce obesity in Britain | Letter
stories.summary: A British health select committee report finds obesity is an engineered outcome, not a failure of willpower. The report highlights a flaw in decades of British food policy, specifically the "high in fat, salt or sugar" framework, which unfairly targets fat as a primary driver of weight gain. The committee recommends targeting ultra-processed foods high in sugar and refined carbohydrates instead. This shift in focus could lead to more effective public health measures.
